summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs scope with install-time host reconnaissance and exfiltration
details: Package name impersonates the official @solana-labs npm scope (the legitimate Solana Labs package is `@coral-xyz/anchor`; `ancor` is a misspelling of `anchor`). On install, package.json declares `"postinstall": "node install.js"`, which automatically executes install.js during `npm install`. install.js reads host identifiers (hostname captured at multiple points around lines 10/25/27), invokes `curl` (line 103) and performs an HTTP POST (line 46) to send collected host information to an external endpoint. The combination of scope-impersonation, automatic postinstall execution, host-identity collection, and outbound POST/curl exfiltration matches the supply-chain reconnaissance/exfiltration fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
